targetTranslation: they write in lines (referring to angels recording deeds)
ayahArabic: نٓ وَٱلْقَلَمِ وَمَا يَسْطُرُونَ
ayahEnglish: Nūn. By the pen and what they inscribe,

ayahArabic: مَآ أَنتَ بِنِعْمَةِ رَبِّكَ بِمَجْنُونٍ
ayahEnglish: You are not, [O Muḥammad], by the favor of your Lord, a madman.

ayahArabic: مَآ أَنتَ بِنِعْمَةِ رَبِّكَ بِمَجْنُونٍ
ayahEnglish: You are not, [O Muḥammad], by the favor of your Lord, a madman.

targetTranslation: other than, different from (describes the reward أَجْرًا)
ayahArabic: وَإِنَّ لَكَ لَأَجْرًا غَيْرَ مَمْنُونٍ
ayahEnglish: And indeed, for you is a reward uninterrupted.

ayahArabic: وَإِنَّ لَكَ لَأَجْرًا غَيْرَ مَمْنُونٍ
ayahEnglish: And indeed, for you is a reward uninterrupted.

ayahArabic: وَإِنَّكَ لَعَلَىٰ خُلُقٍ عَظِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: And indeed, you are of a great moral character.

ayahArabic: وَإِنَّكَ لَعَلَىٰ خُلُقٍ عَظِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: And indeed, you are of a great moral character.

ayahArabic: إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَن ضَلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِٱلْمُهْتَدِينَ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who has gone astray from His way, and He is most knowing of the [rightly] guided.

ayahArabic: إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَن ضَلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِٱلْمُهْتَدِينَ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who has gone astray from His way, and He is most knowing of the [rightly] guided.

ayahArabic: إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَن ضَلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِٱلْمُهْتَدِينَ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who has gone astray from His way, and He is most knowing of the [rightly] guided.

targetTranslation: he lost the right way (subject of أَعْلَمُ)
ayahArabic: إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَن ضَلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِٱلْمُهْتَدِينَ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who has gone astray from His way, and He is most knowing of the [rightly] guided.

ayahArabic: إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَن ضَلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِٱلْمُهْتَدِينَ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who has gone astray from His way, and He is most knowing of the [rightly] guided.

ayahArabic: إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَن ضَلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِٱلْمُهْتَدِينَ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who has gone astray from His way, and He is most knowing of the [rightly] guided.

ayahArabic: فَلَا تُطِعِ ٱلْمُكَذِّبِينَ
ayahEnglish: Then do not obey the deniers.

ayahArabic: وَدُّوا۟ لَوْ تُدْهِنُ فَيُدْهِنُونَ
ayahEnglish: They wish that you would soften [in your position], so they would soften [toward you].

ayahArabic: وَدُّوا۟ لَوْ تُدْهِنُ فَيُدْهِنُونَ
ayahEnglish: They wish that you would soften [in your position], so they would soften [toward you].

targetTranslation: you obey (command: do not!) (connected to وَلَا)
ayahArabic: وَلَا تُطِعْ كُلَّ حَلَّافٍ مَّهِينٍ
ayahEnglish: And do not obey every worthless habitual swearer

targetTranslation: every (object of لَا تُطِعْ - do not obey)
ayahArabic: وَلَا تُطِعْ كُلَّ حَلَّافٍ مَّهِينٍ
ayahEnglish: And do not obey every worthless habitual swearer

ayahArabic: وَلَا تُطِعْ كُلَّ حَلَّافٍ مَّهِينٍ
ayahEnglish: And do not obey every worthless habitual swearer

ayahArabic: وَلَا تُطِعْ كُلَّ حَلَّافٍ مَّهِينٍ
ayahEnglish: And do not obey every worthless habitual swearer

ayahArabic: هَمَّازٍ مَّشَّآءٍۭ بِنَمِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: [And] scorner, going about with malicious gossip -

ayahArabic: هَمَّازٍ مَّشَّآءٍۭ بِنَمِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: [And] scorner, going about with malicious gossip -

ayahArabic: مَّنَّاعٍ لِّلْخَيْرِ مُعْتَدٍ أَثِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: A preventer of good, transgressing and sinful,

ayahArabic: مَّنَّاعٍ لِّلْخَيْرِ مُعْتَدٍ أَثِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: A preventer of good, transgressing and sinful,

targetTranslation: an intensely sinful one (describing مَّنَّاعٍ - a preventer)
ayahArabic: مَّنَّاعٍ لِّلْخَيْرِ مُعْتَدٍ أَثِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: A preventer of good, transgressing and sinful,

targetTranslation: a very harsh person (describing someone in a negative way)
ayahArabic: عُتُلٍّۭ بَعْدَ ذٰلِكَ زَنِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: Cruel, moreover, and an illegitimate pretender.

ayahArabic: عُتُلٍّۭ بَعْدَ ذٰلِكَ زَنِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: Cruel, moreover, and an illegitimate pretender.

ayahArabic: عُتُلٍّۭ بَعْدَ ذٰلِكَ زَنِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: Cruel, moreover, and an illegitimate pretender.

targetTranslation: a marked/branded one (described alongside عُتُلٍّ/harsh person)
ayahArabic: عُتُلٍّۭ بَعْدَ ذٰلِكَ زَنِيمٍ
ayahEnglish: Cruel, moreover, and an illegitimate pretender.

ayahArabic: أَن كَانَ ذَا مَالٍ وَبَنِينَ
ayahEnglish: Because he is a possessor of wealth and children,

ayahArabic: أَن كَانَ ذَا مَالٍ وَبَنِينَ
ayahEnglish: Because he is a possessor of wealth and children,

ayahArabic: أَن كَانَ ذَا مَالٍ وَبَنِينَ
ayahEnglish: Because he is a possessor of wealth and children,

targetTranslation: whenever (something happens in the future) (introduces تُتْلَىٰ - is recited)
ayahArabic: إِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِ ءَايٰتُنَا قَالَ أَسٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ
ayahEnglish: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, "Legends of the former peoples."

targetTranslation: it is recited (passive present tense of آيَاتُنَا)
ayahArabic: إِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِ ءَايٰتُنَا قَالَ أَسٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ
ayahEnglish: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, "Legends of the former peoples."

targetTranslation: our signs/verses (subject of تُتْلَى - are recited)
ayahArabic: إِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِ ءَايٰتُنَا قَالَ أَسٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ
ayahEnglish: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, "Legends of the former peoples."

ayahArabic: إِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِ ءَايٰتُنَا قَالَ أَسٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ
ayahEnglish: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, "Legends of the former peoples."

ayahArabic: إِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِ ءَايٰتُنَا قَالَ أَسٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ
ayahEnglish: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, "Legends of the former peoples."

ayahArabic: إِنَّا بَلَوْنٰهُمْ كَمَا بَلَوْنَآ أَصْحٰبَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ إِذْ أَقْسَمُوا۟ لَيَصْرِمُنَّهَا مُصْبِحِينَ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, We have tried them as We tried the companions of the garden, when they swore to cut its fruit in the [early] morning

ayahArabic: إِنَّا بَلَوْنٰهُمْ كَمَا بَلَوْنَآ أَصْحٰبَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ إِذْ أَقْسَمُوا۟ لَيَصْرِمُنَّهَا مُصْبِحِينَ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, We have tried them as We tried the companions of the garden, when they swore to cut its fruit in the [early] morning

ayahArabic: إِنَّا بَلَوْنٰهُمْ كَمَا بَلَوْنَآ أَصْحٰبَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ إِذْ أَقْسَمُوا۟ لَيَصْرِمُنَّهَا مُصْبِحِينَ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, We have tried them as We tried the companions of the garden, when they swore to cut its fruit in the [early] morning

ayahArabic: إِنَّا بَلَوْنٰهُمْ كَمَا بَلَوْنَآ أَصْحٰبَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ إِذْ أَقْسَمُوا۟ لَيَصْرِمُنَّهَا مُصْبِحِينَ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, We have tried them as We tried the companions of the garden, when they swore to cut its fruit in the [early] morning

ayahArabic: إِنَّا بَلَوْنٰهُمْ كَمَا بَلَوْنَآ أَصْحٰبَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ إِذْ أَقْسَمُوا۟ لَيَصْرِمُنَّهَا مُصْبِحِينَ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, We have tried them as We tried the companions of the garden, when they swore to cut its fruit in the [early] morning

targetTranslation: while they were entering morning time (describes their state when they swore أَقْسَمُوا)
ayahArabic: إِنَّا بَلَوْنٰهُمْ كَمَا بَلَوْنَآ أَصْحٰبَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ إِذْ أَقْسَمُوا۟ لَيَصْرِمُنَّهَا مُصْبِحِينَ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, We have tried them as We tried the companions of the garden, when they swore to cut its fruit in the [early] morning

ayahArabic: وَلَا يَسْتَثْنُونَ
ayahEnglish: Without making exception.

targetTranslation: a circler/one who goes around (subject of فَطَافَ)
ayahArabic: فَطَافَ عَلَيْهَا طَآئِفٌ مِّن رَّبِّكَ وَهُمْ نَآئِمُونَ
ayahEnglish: So there came upon it [i.e., the garden] an affliction from your Lord while they were asleep.

ayahArabic: فَطَافَ عَلَيْهَا طَآئِفٌ مِّن رَّبِّكَ وَهُمْ نَآئِمُونَ
ayahEnglish: So there came upon it [i.e., the garden] an affliction from your Lord while they were asleep.

ayahArabic: فَطَافَ عَلَيْهَا طَآئِفٌ مِّن رَّبِّكَ وَهُمْ نَآئِمُونَ
ayahEnglish: So there came upon it [i.e., the garden] an affliction from your Lord while they were asleep.

targetTranslation: those who enter morning time (subject of فَتَنَادَوْا۟)
ayahArabic: فَتَنَادَوْا۟ مُصْبِحِينَ
ayahEnglish: And they called one another at morning,

ayahArabic: أَنِ ٱغْدُوا۟ عَلَىٰ حَرْثِكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ صٰرِمِينَ
ayahEnglish: [Saying], "Go early to your crop if you would cut the fruit."

ayahArabic: أَنِ ٱغْدُوا۟ عَلَىٰ حَرْثِكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ صٰرِمِينَ
ayahEnglish: [Saying], "Go early to your crop if you would cut the fruit."

ayahArabic: أَنِ ٱغْدُوا۟ عَلَىٰ حَرْثِكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ صٰرِمِينَ
ayahEnglish: [Saying], "Go early to your crop if you would cut the fruit."

ayahArabic: أَنِ ٱغْدُوا۟ عَلَىٰ حَرْثِكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ صٰرِمِينَ
ayahEnglish: [Saying], "Go early to your crop if you would cut the fruit."

targetTranslation: they whisper to one another (subject of وَهُمْ)
ayahArabic: فَٱنطَلَقُوا۟ وَهُمْ يَتَخٰفَتُونَ
ayahEnglish: So they set out, while lowering their voices,

ayahArabic: أَن لَّا يَدْخُلَنَّهَا ٱلْيَوْمَ عَلَيْكُم مِّسْكِينٌ
ayahEnglish: [Saying], "There will surely not enter it today upon you [any] poor person."

ayahArabic: أَن لَّا يَدْخُلَنَّهَا ٱلْيَوْمَ عَلَيْكُم مِّسْكِينٌ
ayahEnglish: [Saying], "There will surely not enter it today upon you [any] poor person."

ayahArabic: وَغَدَوْا۟ عَلَىٰ حَرْدٍ قٰدِرِينَ
ayahEnglish: And they went early in determination, [assuming themselves] able.

targetTranslation: ones having power (subject of غَدَوْا - they went forth)
ayahArabic: وَغَدَوْا۟ عَلَىٰ حَرْدٍ قٰدِرِينَ
ayahEnglish: And they went early in determination, [assuming themselves] able.

ayahArabic: فَلَمَّا رَأَوْهَا قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا لَضَآلُّونَ
ayahEnglish: But when they saw it, they said, "Indeed, we are lost;

targetTranslation: they said (after seeing it فَلَمَّا رَأَوْهَا)
ayahArabic: فَلَمَّا رَأَوْهَا قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا لَضَآلُّونَ
ayahEnglish: But when they saw it, they said, "Indeed, we are lost;

ayahArabic: بَلْ نَحْنُ مَحْرُومُونَ
ayahEnglish: Rather, we have been deprived."

ayahArabic: بَلْ نَحْنُ مَحْرُومُونَ
ayahEnglish: Rather, we have been deprived."

ayahArabic: قَالَ أَوْسَطُهُمْ أَلَمْ أَقُل لَّكُمْ لَوْلَا تُسَبِّحُونَ
ayahEnglish: The most moderate of them said, "Did I not say to you, 'Why do you not exalt [Allāh]?'"

targetTranslation: their most moderate one (the one speaking in قَالَ)
ayahArabic: قَالَ أَوْسَطُهُمْ أَلَمْ أَقُل لَّكُمْ لَوْلَا تُسَبِّحُونَ
ayahEnglish: The most moderate of them said, "Did I not say to you, 'Why do you not exalt [Allāh]?'"

ayahArabic: قَالَ أَوْسَطُهُمْ أَلَمْ أَقُل لَّكُمْ لَوْلَا تُسَبِّحُونَ
ayahEnglish: The most moderate of them said, "Did I not say to you, 'Why do you not exalt [Allāh]?'"

ayahArabic: قَالَ أَوْسَطُهُمْ أَلَمْ أَقُل لَّكُمْ لَوْلَا تُسَبِّحُونَ
ayahEnglish: The most moderate of them said, "Did I not say to you, 'Why do you not exalt [Allāh]?'"

ayahArabic: قَالَ أَوْسَطُهُمْ أَلَمْ أَقُل لَّكُمْ لَوْلَا تُسَبِّحُونَ
ayahEnglish: The most moderate of them said, "Did I not say to you, 'Why do you not exalt [Allāh]?'"

ayahArabic: قَالُوا۟ سُبْحٰنَ رَبِّنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا ظٰلِمِينَ
ayahEnglish: They said, "Exalted is our Lord! Indeed, we were wrongdoers."

targetTranslation: perfect is, glorified is (used before رَبِّنَآ)
ayahArabic: قَالُوا۟ سُبْحٰنَ رَبِّنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا ظٰلِمِينَ
ayahEnglish: They said, "Exalted is our Lord! Indeed, we were wrongdoers."

targetTranslation: our Lord (addressing Allah in سُبْحٰنَ رَبِّنَآ - Glory be to our Lord)
ayahArabic: قَالُوا۟ سُبْحٰنَ رَبِّنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا ظٰلِمِينَ
ayahEnglish: They said, "Exalted is our Lord! Indeed, we were wrongdoers."

ayahArabic: قَالُوا۟ سُبْحٰنَ رَبِّنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا ظٰلِمِينَ
ayahEnglish: They said, "Exalted is our Lord! Indeed, we were wrongdoers."

ayahArabic: قَالُوا۟ سُبْحٰنَ رَبِّنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا ظٰلِمِينَ
ayahEnglish: They said, "Exalted is our Lord! Indeed, we were wrongdoers."

ayahArabic: فَأَقْبَلَ بَعْضُهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ يَتَلٰوَمُونَ
ayahEnglish: Then they approached one another, blaming each other.

ayahArabic: فَأَقْبَلَ بَعْضُهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ يَتَلٰوَمُونَ
ayahEnglish: Then they approached one another, blaming each other.

ayahArabic: فَأَقْبَلَ بَعْضُهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ يَتَلٰوَمُونَ
ayahEnglish: Then they approached one another, blaming each other.

targetTranslation: they said (to each other: يٰوَيْلَنَآ - 'O woe to us!')
ayahArabic: قَالُوا۟ يٰوَيْلَنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا طٰغِينَ
ayahEnglish: They said, "O woe to us; indeed we were transgressors.

ayahArabic: قَالُوا۟ يٰوَيْلَنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا طٰغِينَ
ayahEnglish: They said, "O woe to us; indeed we were transgressors.

targetTranslation: ones who go beyond limits (describes كُنَّا - 'we were')
ayahArabic: قَالُوا۟ يٰوَيْلَنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا طٰغِينَ
ayahEnglish: They said, "O woe to us; indeed we were transgressors.

targetTranslation: perhaps, it may be that (introduces a hopeful statement about رَبُّنَآ)
ayahArabic: عَسَىٰ رَبُّنَآ أَن يُبْدِلَنَا خَيْرًا مِّنْهَآ إِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا رٰغِبُونَ
ayahEnglish: Perhaps our Lord will substitute for us [one] better than it. Indeed, we are toward our Lord desirous."

ayahArabic: عَسَىٰ رَبُّنَآ أَن يُبْدِلَنَا خَيْرًا مِّنْهَآ إِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا رٰغِبُونَ
ayahEnglish: Perhaps our Lord will substitute for us [one] better than it. Indeed, we are toward our Lord desirous."

ayahArabic: عَسَىٰ رَبُّنَآ أَن يُبْدِلَنَا خَيْرًا مِّنْهَآ إِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا رٰغِبُونَ
ayahEnglish: Perhaps our Lord will substitute for us [one] better than it. Indeed, we are toward our Lord desirous."

targetTranslation: something better (object of يُبْدِلَنَا - may He exchange for us)
ayahArabic: عَسَىٰ رَبُّنَآ أَن يُبْدِلَنَا خَيْرًا مِّنْهَآ إِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا رٰغِبُونَ
ayahEnglish: Perhaps our Lord will substitute for us [one] better than it. Indeed, we are toward our Lord desirous."

ayahArabic: عَسَىٰ رَبُّنَآ أَن يُبْدِلَنَا خَيْرًا مِّنْهَآ إِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا رٰغِبُونَ
ayahEnglish: Perhaps our Lord will substitute for us [one] better than it. Indeed, we are toward our Lord desirous."

targetTranslation: those who eagerly desire (plural, in nominative case)
ayahArabic: عَسَىٰ رَبُّنَآ أَن يُبْدِلَنَا خَيْرًا مِّنْهَآ إِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا رٰغِبُونَ
ayahEnglish: Perhaps our Lord will substitute for us [one] better than it. Indeed, we are toward our Lord desirous."

targetTranslation: greater/bigger (than the punishment mentioned in العَذَابُ)
ayahArabic: كَذٰلِكَ ٱلْعَذَابُ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ أَكْبَرُ لَوْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْلَمُونَ
ayahEnglish: Such is the punishment [of this world]. And the punishment of the Hereafter is greater, if they only knew.

ayahArabic: كَذٰلِكَ ٱلْعَذَابُ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ أَكْبَرُ لَوْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْلَمُونَ
ayahEnglish: Such is the punishment [of this world]. And the punishment of the Hereafter is greater, if they only knew.

ayahArabic: كَذٰلِكَ ٱلْعَذَابُ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ أَكْبَرُ لَوْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْلَمُونَ
ayahEnglish: Such is the punishment [of this world]. And the punishment of the Hereafter is greater, if they only knew.

ayahArabic: إِنَّ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ جَنَّاتِ ٱلنَّعِيمِ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, for the righteous with their Lord are the Gardens of Pleasure.

targetTranslation: their Lord (the one who nurtures and cares for them)
ayahArabic: إِنَّ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ جَنَّاتِ ٱلنَّعِيمِ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, for the righteous with their Lord are the Gardens of Pleasure.

ayahArabic: إِنَّ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ جَنَّاتِ ٱلنَّعِيمِ
ayahEnglish: Indeed, for the righteous with their Lord are the Gardens of Pleasure.

ayahArabic: أَفَنَجْعَلُ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ كَٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ
ayahEnglish: Then will We treat the Muslims like the criminals?

targetTranslation: what (is wrong with you in كَيْفَ تَحْكُمُونَ)
ayahArabic: مَا لَكُمْ كَيْفَ تَحْكُمُونَ
ayahEnglish: What is [the matter] with you? How do you judge?

ayahArabic: مَا لَكُمْ كَيْفَ تَحْكُمُونَ
ayahEnglish: What is [the matter] with you? How do you judge?

ayahArabic: مَا لَكُمْ كَيْفَ تَحْكُمُونَ
ayahEnglish: What is [the matter] with you? How do you judge?

ayahArabic: أَمْ لَكُمْ كِتٰبٌ فِيهِ تَدْرُسُونَ
ayahEnglish: Or do you have a scripture in which you learn

ayahArabic: أَمْ لَكُمْ كِتٰبٌ فِيهِ تَدْرُسُونَ
ayahEnglish: Or do you have a scripture in which you learn

targetTranslation: you all choose carefully for yourselves
ayahArabic: إِنَّ لَكُمْ فِيهِ لَمَا تَخَيَّرُونَ
ayahEnglish: That indeed for you is whatever you choose?

ayahArabic: أَمْ لَكُمْ أَيْمٰنٌ عَلَيْنَا بٰلِغَةٌ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيٰمَةِ إِنَّ لَكُمْ لَمَا تَحْكُمُونَ
ayahEnglish: Or do you have oaths [binding] upon Us, extending until the Day of Resurrection, that indeed for you is whatever you judge?

targetTranslation: a reaching/effective one (feminine, subject of oath)
ayahArabic: أَمْ لَكُمْ أَيْمٰنٌ عَلَيْنَا بٰلِغَةٌ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيٰمَةِ إِنَّ لَكُمْ لَمَا تَحْكُمُونَ
ayahEnglish: Or do you have oaths [binding] upon Us, extending until the Day of Resurrection, that indeed for you is whatever you judge?

ayahArabic: أَمْ لَكُمْ أَيْمٰنٌ عَلَيْنَا بٰلِغَةٌ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيٰمَةِ إِنَّ لَكُمْ لَمَا تَحْكُمُونَ
ayahEnglish: Or do you have oaths [binding] upon Us, extending until the Day of Resurrection, that indeed for you is whatever you judge?

ayahArabic: أَمْ لَكُمْ أَيْمٰنٌ عَلَيْنَا بٰلِغَةٌ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيٰمَةِ إِنَّ لَكُمْ لَمَا تَحْكُمُونَ
ayahEnglish: Or do you have oaths [binding] upon Us, extending until the Day of Resurrection, that indeed for you is whatever you judge?

targetTranslation: ask them! (command to ask the people being discussed)
ayahArabic: سَلْهُمْ أَيُّهُم بِذٰلِكَ زَعِيمٌ
ayahEnglish: Ask them which of them, for that [claim], is responsible.

targetTranslation: which of them (asking about who among them in سَلْهُمْ)
ayahArabic: سَلْهُمْ أَيُّهُم بِذٰلِكَ زَعِيمٌ
ayahEnglish: Ask them which of them, for that [claim], is responsible.

ayahArabic: سَلْهُمْ أَيُّهُم بِذٰلِكَ زَعِيمٌ
ayahEnglish: Ask them which of them, for that [claim], is responsible.

ayahArabic: أَمْ لَهُمْ شُرَكَآءُ فَلْيَأْتُوا۟ بِشُرَكَآئِهِمْ إِن كَانُوا۟ صٰدِقِينَ
ayahEnglish: Or do they have partners? Then let them bring their partners, if they should be truthful.

ayahArabic: أَمْ لَهُمْ شُرَكَآءُ فَلْيَأْتُوا۟ بِشُرَكَآئِهِمْ إِن كَانُوا۟ صٰدِقِينَ
ayahEnglish: Or do they have partners? Then let them bring their partners, if they should be truthful.

ayahArabic: أَمْ لَهُمْ شُرَكَآءُ فَلْيَأْتُوا۟ بِشُرَكَآئِهِمْ إِن كَانُوا۟ صٰدِقِينَ
ayahEnglish: Or do they have partners? Then let them bring their partners, if they should be truthful.

ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يُكْشَفُ عَن سَاقٍ وَيُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ فَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ
ayahEnglish: The Day the shin will be uncovered and they are invited to prostration but they [i.e., the disbelievers] will not be able,

targetTranslation: is being uncovered (on the Day when يَوْمَ)
ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يُكْشَفُ عَن سَاقٍ وَيُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ فَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ
ayahEnglish: The Day the shin will be uncovered and they are invited to prostration but they [i.e., the disbelievers] will not be able,

ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يُكْشَفُ عَن سَاقٍ وَيُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ فَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ
ayahEnglish: The Day the shin will be uncovered and they are invited to prostration but they [i.e., the disbelievers] will not be able,

targetTranslation: they are able (referring to the disbelievers who cannot prostrate)
ayahArabic: يَوْمَ يُكْشَفُ عَن سَاقٍ وَيُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ فَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ
ayahEnglish: The Day the shin will be uncovered and they are invited to prostration but they [i.e., the disbelievers] will not be able,

targetTranslation: being humble (feminine, describes the state of أَبْصٰر/eyes)
ayahArabic: خٰشِعَةً أَبْصٰرُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ وَقَدْ كَانُوا۟ يُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ وَهُمْ سٰلِمُونَ
ayahEnglish: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. And they used to be invited to prostration while they were sound.

ayahArabic: خٰشِعَةً أَبْصٰرُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ وَقَدْ كَانُوا۟ يُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ وَهُمْ سٰلِمُونَ
ayahEnglish: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. And they used to be invited to prostration while they were sound.

ayahArabic: خٰشِعَةً أَبْصٰرُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ وَقَدْ كَانُوا۟ يُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ وَهُمْ سٰلِمُونَ
ayahEnglish: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. And they used to be invited to prostration while they were sound.

ayahArabic: خٰشِعَةً أَبْصٰرُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ وَقَدْ كَانُوا۟ يُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ وَهُمْ سٰلِمُونَ
ayahEnglish: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. And they used to be invited to prostration while they were sound.

ayahArabic: خٰشِعَةً أَبْصٰرُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ وَقَدْ كَانُوا۟ يُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ وَهُمْ سٰلِمُونَ
ayahEnglish: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. And they used to be invited to prostration while they were sound.

ayahArabic: خٰشِعَةً أَبْصٰرُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ وَقَدْ كَانُوا۟ يُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ وَهُمْ سٰلِمُونَ
ayahEnglish: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. And they used to be invited to prostration while they were sound.

ayahArabic: خٰشِعَةً أَبْصٰرُهُمْ تَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ وَقَدْ كَانُوا۟ يُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ وَهُمْ سٰلِمُونَ
ayahEnglish: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. And they used to be invited to prostration while they were sound.

ayahArabic: فَذَرْنِى وَمَن يُكَذِّبُ بِهٰذَا ٱلْحَدِيثِ سَنَسْتَدْرِجُهُم مِّنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
ayahEnglish: So leave Me, [O Muḥammad], with [the matter of] whoever denies this statement [i.e., the Qur’ān]. We will progressively lead them [to punishment] from where they do not know.

ayahArabic: فَذَرْنِى وَمَن يُكَذِّبُ بِهٰذَا ٱلْحَدِيثِ سَنَسْتَدْرِجُهُم مِّنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
ayahEnglish: So leave Me, [O Muḥammad], with [the matter of] whoever denies this statement [i.e., the Qur’ān]. We will progressively lead them [to punishment] from where they do not know.

targetTranslation: they know (referring to the disbelievers in the ayah)
ayahArabic: فَذَرْنِى وَمَن يُكَذِّبُ بِهٰذَا ٱلْحَدِيثِ سَنَسْتَدْرِجُهُم مِّنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
ayahEnglish: So leave Me, [O Muḥammad], with [the matter of] whoever denies this statement [i.e., the Qur’ān]. We will progressively lead them [to punishment] from where they do not know.

ayahArabic: وَأُمْلِى لَهُمْ إِنَّ كَيْدِى مَتِينٌ
ayahEnglish: And I will give them time. Indeed, My plan is firm.

targetTranslation: very strong and firm (describing Allah's might)
ayahArabic: وَأُمْلِى لَهُمْ إِنَّ كَيْدِى مَتِينٌ
ayahEnglish: And I will give them time. Indeed, My plan is firm.

ayahArabic: أَمْ تَسْـَٔلُهُمْ أَجْرًا فَهُم مِّن مَّغْرَمٍ مُّثْقَلُونَ
ayahEnglish: Or do you ask of them a payment, so they are by debt burdened down?

targetTranslation: a payment, a reward (object of تَسْـَٔلُ/you ask)
ayahArabic: أَمْ تَسْـَٔلُهُمْ أَجْرًا فَهُم مِّن مَّغْرَمٍ مُّثْقَلُونَ
ayahEnglish: Or do you ask of them a payment, so they are by debt burdened down?

targetTranslation: a debt/burden (that is weighing them down)
ayahArabic: أَمْ تَسْـَٔلُهُمْ أَجْرًا فَهُم مِّن مَّغْرَمٍ مُّثْقَلُونَ
ayahEnglish: Or do you ask of them a payment, so they are by debt burdened down?

targetTranslation: those who are burdened (by debt in أَجْرًا)
ayahArabic: أَمْ تَسْـَٔلُهُمْ أَجْرًا فَهُم مِّن مَّغْرَمٍ مُّثْقَلُونَ
ayahEnglish: Or do you ask of them a payment, so they are by debt burdened down?

ayahArabic: أَمْ عِندَهُمُ ٱلْغَيْبُ فَهُمْ يَكْتُبُونَ
ayahEnglish: Or have they [knowledge of] the unseen, so they write [it] down?

ayahArabic: أَمْ عِندَهُمُ ٱلْغَيْبُ فَهُمْ يَكْتُبُونَ
ayahEnglish: Or have they [knowledge of] the unseen, so they write [it] down?

ayahArabic: فَٱصْبِرْ لِحُكْمِ رَبِّكَ وَلَا تَكُن كَصَاحِبِ ٱلْحُوتِ إِذْ نَادَىٰ وَهُوَ مَكْظُومٌ
ayahEnglish: Then be patient for the decision of your Lord, [O Muḥammad], and be not like the companion of the fish [i.e., Jonah] when he called out while he was distressed.

ayahArabic: فَٱصْبِرْ لِحُكْمِ رَبِّكَ وَلَا تَكُن كَصَاحِبِ ٱلْحُوتِ إِذْ نَادَىٰ وَهُوَ مَكْظُومٌ
ayahEnglish: Then be patient for the decision of your Lord, [O Muḥammad], and be not like the companion of the fish [i.e., Jonah] when he called out while he was distressed.

ayahArabic: فَٱصْبِرْ لِحُكْمِ رَبِّكَ وَلَا تَكُن كَصَاحِبِ ٱلْحُوتِ إِذْ نَادَىٰ وَهُوَ مَكْظُومٌ
ayahEnglish: Then be patient for the decision of your Lord, [O Muḥammad], and be not like the companion of the fish [i.e., Jonah] when he called out while he was distressed.

targetTranslation: he called out to someone/called with someone
ayahArabic: فَٱصْبِرْ لِحُكْمِ رَبِّكَ وَلَا تَكُن كَصَاحِبِ ٱلْحُوتِ إِذْ نَادَىٰ وَهُوَ مَكْظُومٌ
ayahEnglish: Then be patient for the decision of your Lord, [O Muḥammad], and be not like the companion of the fish [i.e., Jonah] when he called out while he was distressed.

ayahArabic: فَٱصْبِرْ لِحُكْمِ رَبِّكَ وَلَا تَكُن كَصَاحِبِ ٱلْحُوتِ إِذْ نَادَىٰ وَهُوَ مَكْظُومٌ
ayahEnglish: Then be patient for the decision of your Lord, [O Muḥammad], and be not like the companion of the fish [i.e., Jonah] when he called out while he was distressed.

targetTranslation: it reached him just in time (referring to نِعْمَةٌ reaching him)
ayahArabic: لَّوْلَآ أَن تَدٰرَكَهُۥ نِعْمَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ لَنُبِذَ بِٱلْعَرَآءِ وَهُوَ مَذْمُومٌ
ayahEnglish: If not that a favor [i.e., mercy] from his Lord overtook him, he would have been thrown onto the naked shore while he was censured.

ayahArabic: لَّوْلَآ أَن تَدٰرَكَهُۥ نِعْمَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ لَنُبِذَ بِٱلْعَرَآءِ وَهُوَ مَذْمُومٌ
ayahEnglish: If not that a favor [i.e., mercy] from his Lord overtook him, he would have been thrown onto the naked shore while he was censured.

ayahArabic: لَّوْلَآ أَن تَدٰرَكَهُۥ نِعْمَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ لَنُبِذَ بِٱلْعَرَآءِ وَهُوَ مَذْمُومٌ
ayahEnglish: If not that a favor [i.e., mercy] from his Lord overtook him, he would have been thrown onto the naked shore while he was censured.

ayahArabic: لَّوْلَآ أَن تَدٰرَكَهُۥ نِعْمَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ لَنُبِذَ بِٱلْعَرَآءِ وَهُوَ مَذْمُومٌ
ayahEnglish: If not that a favor [i.e., mercy] from his Lord overtook him, he would have been thrown onto the naked shore while he was censured.

targetTranslation: his Lord (the one who chose him)
ayahArabic: فَٱجْتَبٰهُ رَبُّهُۥ فَجَعَلَهُۥ مِنَ ٱلصَّالِحِينَ
ayahEnglish: And his Lord chose him and made him of the righteous.

ayahArabic: وَإِن يَكَادُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَيُزْلِقُونَكَ بِأَبْصٰرِهِمْ لَمَّا سَمِعُوا۟ ٱلذِّكْرَ وَيَقُولُونَ إِنَّهُۥ لَمَجْنُونٌ
ayahEnglish: And indeed, those who disbelieve would almost make you slip with their eyes [i.e., looks] when they hear the message, and they say, "Indeed, he is mad."

targetTranslation: those who - points to specific people or things
ayahArabic: وَإِن يَكَادُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَيُزْلِقُونَكَ بِأَبْصٰرِهِمْ لَمَّا سَمِعُوا۟ ٱلذِّكْرَ وَيَقُولُونَ إِنَّهُۥ لَمَجْنُونٌ
ayahEnglish: And indeed, those who disbelieve would almost make you slip with their eyes [i.e., looks] when they hear the message, and they say, "Indeed, he is mad."

targetTranslation: they disbelieved (subject of لَيُزْلِقُونَكَ - they would make you slip)
ayahArabic: وَإِن يَكَادُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَيُزْلِقُونَكَ بِأَبْصٰرِهِمْ لَمَّا سَمِعُوا۟ ٱلذِّكْرَ وَيَقُولُونَ إِنَّهُۥ لَمَجْنُونٌ
ayahEnglish: And indeed, those who disbelieve would almost make you slip with their eyes [i.e., looks] when they hear the message, and they say, "Indeed, he is mad."

ayahArabic: وَإِن يَكَادُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَيُزْلِقُونَكَ بِأَبْصٰرِهِمْ لَمَّا سَمِعُوا۟ ٱلذِّكْرَ وَيَقُولُونَ إِنَّهُۥ لَمَجْنُونٌ
ayahEnglish: And indeed, those who disbelieve would almost make you slip with their eyes [i.e., looks] when they hear the message, and they say, "Indeed, he is mad."

ayahArabic: وَإِن يَكَادُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَيُزْلِقُونَكَ بِأَبْصٰرِهِمْ لَمَّا سَمِعُوا۟ ٱلذِّكْرَ وَيَقُولُونَ إِنَّهُۥ لَمَجْنُونٌ
ayahEnglish: And indeed, those who disbelieve would almost make you slip with their eyes [i.e., looks] when they hear the message, and they say, "Indeed, he is mad."

ayahArabic: وَمَا هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرٌ لِّلْعٰلَمِينَ
ayahEnglish: But it is not except a reminder to the worlds.

targetTranslation: a reminder/remembrance (that comes before لِّلْعٰلَمِينَ - for all the worlds)
ayahArabic: وَمَا هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرٌ لِّلْعٰلَمِينَ
ayahEnglish: But it is not except a reminder to the worlds.
